mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in retail sales signals strengthening household demand and near-term earnings expectations, so higher RSAFS growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than the unconditional base rate over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS after a prior plateau lowers discount rates and supports equity duration, so a recent policy-easing signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls by at least 0.25pp over the prior 3 monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if triggered easing observations have mean 60-day S&P 500 returns less than or equal to non-triggered observations across two consecutive 5-year walk-forward samples.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment indicates deteriorating labor income and recession risk, so an increase in UNRATE predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over the prior 3 monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE-rise triggers are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered instances over a 10-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPI while FEDFUNDS is not falling raises expected real-rate pressure and reduces valuation support, so the joint inflation-policy configuration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds its 12-month growth and FEDFUNDS 3-month change is greater than or equal to 0
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term has non-negative 20-day return spread, or if its Rank IC is below 0.03 in every rolling 3-year validation window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent industrial production declines signal weakening goods demand and operating leverage, so falling INDPRO predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls in at least 2 of the prior 3 monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO contraction months have higher average 60-day S&P 500 returns than expansion months in at least 70% of walk-forward folds.

mechanism_narrative: A breakout in housing starts indicates improving construction activity and credit-sensitive demand, so stronger HOUST momentum predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above its prior 12-month average after being below that average in the previous monthly observation
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST upside crossovers do not produce a positive 60-day return hit rate above 55% over the full walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll gains combined with low unemployment signal durable wage income without immediate labor-market stress, so the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE strength signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases over the prior monthly observation and UNRATE is less than or equal to 4.0
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-low interaction has a 5-day forward return hit rate below the unconditional hit rate in two consecutive 4-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP expansion with stable or falling policy rates indicates growth support without tighter discount rates, so the joint GDPC1-FEDFUNDS configuration predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 latest quarterly growth is positive and FEDFUNDS 3-month change is less than or equal to 0
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have median 60-day S&P 500 returns less than zero or below the median return of non-triggered observations in a walk-forward test.
